prompt: How many twin-prime pairs (p, p+2 both prime) are there with 10,000,000 <= p and p+2 < 20,000,000? Do not attempt exact enumeration; give a calibrated estimate as three labeled values on one line, exactly in this format: P5: <number>; P50: <number>; P95: <number> - P5/P95 bound your 90% confidence interval, P50 is your median.
notes: Truth by direct sieve of [0, 2e7) = 48,427 (verify_answers.py). Differential shape: the published below-1e6 twin count (8,169) plus Hardy-Littlewood density scaling gives a close-but-biased anchor -- the interval-width call, not the anchor, decides the outcome.
